narcissistic.en: having an excessive sense of your own importance or attractiveness

ex.en: He was too narcissistic to notice how his behavior affected others.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 자기애가 강해서 자신의 행동이 남에게 미치는 영향을 알아채지 못했다.

narrator.en: a person who tells the story in a book, film, or play

ex.en: The narrator describes events from a child's point of view.
ex.ko: 그 서술자는 사건을 아이의 시각에서 묘사한다.

narrowly.en: by a very small amount; only just

ex.en: She narrowly avoided a car accident on her way to work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 출근길에 자동차 사고를 간신히 피했다.

nationwide.en: happening or existing in all parts of a country

ex.en: The company launched a nationwide advertising campaign.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 전국적인 광고 캠페인을 시작했다.

nationwide.en: throughout the whole of a country

ex.en: The product is now sold nationwide.
ex.ko: 그 제품은 이제 전국적으로 판매된다.

native speaker.en: a person who has spoken a particular language since childhood

ex.en: She sounds like a native speaker of English.
ex.ko: 그녀는 영어 원어민처럼 들린다.

naughty.cue: 아이가 말을 안 듣고 짓궂게 굴 때
naughty.en: (especially of a child) behaving badly or causing trouble in a playful way

ex.en: The naughty puppy chewed up my shoes.
ex.ko: 그 말썽꾸러기 강아지가 내 신발을 물어뜯었다.

ex.en: He served in the naval forces for ten years.
ex.ko: 그는 10년간 해군에서 복무했다.

navigate.en: find the way to get somewhere, using a map, GPS, or by planning a route

ex.en: We used a map to navigate through the unfamiliar city.
ex.ko: 우리는 낯선 도시를 지도를 보며 길을 찾아 다녔다.

navigate.en: deal with a difficult situation or problem skillfully

ex.en: The new manager had to navigate several conflicts within the team.
ex.ko: 새 팀장은 팀 내 여러 갈등을 헤쳐 나가야 했다.

navigation.en: the act of finding the way to a place, or a system that gives directions, as in a ship, plane, or car

ex.en: The car's navigation guided us to the hotel without any trouble.
ex.ko: 차량 내비게이션이 아무 문제 없이 우리를 호텔까지 안내해 주었다.

navy.en: the part of a country's armed forces that fights at sea

ex.en: He joined the navy right after high school.
ex.ko: 그는 고등학교를 졸업하자마자 해군에 입대했다.

ex.en: She wore a navy suit to the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접에 남색 정장을 입고 갔다.

ex.en: The garden was neat and carefully maintained.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 깔끔하고 정성스럽게 관리되어 있었다.

neat.en: (of a strong alcoholic drink) served without ice or water added

ex.en: He always drinks his whiskey neat.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 위스키를 스트레이트로 마신다.

ex.en: That's a neat trick!
ex.ko: 그거 정말 멋진 기술이네!

necessarily.en: in a way that must happen or be true; always

ex.en: Being rich doesn't necessarily mean being happy.
ex.ko: 부자라고 해서 반드시 행복한 것은 아니다.

necessity.en: the fact of being needed or unavoidable

ex.en: There is a growing necessity for renewable energy sources.
ex.ko: 재생 에너지원에 대한 필요성이 커지고 있다.

necessity.en: something that you must have in order to live or do something

ex.en: Clean water is a basic necessity for survival.
ex.ko: 깨끗한 물은 생존을 위한 기본 필수품이다.

needless.en: not necessary and could easily be avoided

ex.en: The delay caused needless stress for everyone.
ex.ko: 그 지연은 모두에게 불필요한 스트레스를 초래했다.

ex.en: The charity provides food for needy families.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 형편이 어려운 가정에 식료품을 제공한다.

needy.en: needing a lot of attention, affection, or reassurance from other people

ex.en: He became needy and insecure after the breakup.
ex.ko: 그는 이별 후 정서적으로 의존적이고 불안해졌다.

negatively.en: in a way that is harmful, critical, or pessimistic

ex.en: The news affected the stock market negatively.
ex.ko: 그 뉴스는 주식 시장에 부정적으로 영향을 미쳤다.

neglect.cue: 돌봐야 할 것을 방치해서 생긴 상태
neglect.en: a lack of care or attention that causes harm

ex.en: The garden was ruined by years of neglect.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 수년간의 방치로 엉망이 되었다.

neglect.en: fail to give enough care or attention to someone or something

ex.en: She neglected her health while working long hours.
ex.ko: 그녀는 장시간 근무하는 동안 자신의 건강을 소홀히 했다.

neighboring.en: next to or near something, especially another country or area

ex.en: Trade between the two neighboring countries has increased.
ex.ko: 그 두 인접국 사이의 무역이 증가했다.

neither.cue: 둘 중 어느 쪽도 아니라고 할 때
neither.ko: 둘 다 아닌, 어느 쪽도 아닌
neither.en: not one and not the other of two people or things

ex.en: Neither answer is correct.
ex.ko: 둘 중 어느 답도 맞지 않다.

neither.cue: 부정문 뒤에 붙여 나도 그렇다고 할 때
neither.en: used to say that a negative statement is also true of another person or thing

ex.en: I don't like coffee, and neither does she.
ex.ko: 나는 커피를 안 좋아하는데, 그녀도 마찬가지다.

nerve.en: one of the thin fibers in the body that carries messages between the brain and other parts of the body

ex.en: The dentist numbed the nerve before the procedure.
ex.ko: 치과의사는 시술 전에 신경을 마취시켰다.

nerve.en: the courage needed to do something difficult or frightening

ex.en: It took a lot of nerve to speak in front of the whole company.
ex.ko: 회사 전체 앞에서 발표하는 데는 상당한 용기가 필요했다.

ex.en: He had the nerve to ask for a raise after being late every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 지각하고도 월급을 올려달라고 할 뻔뻔함이 있었다.

nerves.en: a feeling of anxiety or nervousness, especially before something important

ex.en: She had a fit of nerves before the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험 전에 극도로 긴장했다.

nerves.en: the ability to stay calm in a difficult or dangerous situation

ex.en: You need nerves of steel to be a surgeon.
ex.ko: 외과의사가 되려면 강철 같은 평정심이 필요하다.

nervously.en: in a way that shows fear or anxiety

ex.en: She nervously waited for the results.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초조하게 결과를 기다렸다.

netsurfer.en: a person who spends time looking at different websites on the internet

ex.en: As a dedicated netsurfer, he spends hours browsing different websites every day.
ex.ko: 열성적인 인터넷 서퍼인 그는 매일 몇 시간씩 여러 웹사이트를 둘러본다.

neurosis.en: a mental illness involving high levels of anxiety, worry, or unhappiness, but not a loss of touch with reality

ex.en: Years of stress at work led to his neurosis.
ex.ko: 수년간의 직장 스트레스가 그의 신경증으로 이어졌다.

newscaster.en: a person who reads and presents the news on television or radio

ex.en: The newscaster reported on the storm damage live.
ex.ko: 그 뉴스 진행자는 폭풍 피해를 생방송으로 보도했다.

newsworthy.en: interesting or important enough to be reported as news

ex.en: The scandal was considered highly newsworthy.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 매우 뉴스거리가 되는 것으로 여겨졌다.

nicely.en: in a pleasant, kind, or satisfactory way

ex.en: Everything worked out nicely in the end.
ex.ko: 결국 모든 일이 잘 풀렸다.

nightclub.en: a place that is open late at night where people can dance, drink, and listen to music

ex.en: They danced all night at the nightclub.
ex.ko: 그들은 나이트클럽에서 밤새 춤을 췄다.

ex.en: I woke up sweating after a terrible nightmare.
ex.ko: 나는 끔찍한 악몽을 꾸고 땀에 젖어 깼다.

nightmare.en: a very unpleasant or difficult situation or experience

ex.en: The whole trip turned into a logistical nightmare.
ex.ko: 그 여행 전체가 물류상의 악몽 같은 상황이 되었다.

nobility.en: people of high social rank, especially those with inherited titles

ex.en: The nobility once controlled most of the land.
ex.ko: 귀족들은 한때 대부분의 땅을 지배했다.

nobility.en: the quality of having high moral character or ideals

ex.en: There was a quiet nobility in the way she handled the loss.
ex.ko: 그녀가 그 상실을 대하는 모습에는 조용한 고귀함이 있었다.

noble.en: having very good personal qualities that deserve admiration and respect

ex.en: He made a noble sacrifice for his family.
ex.ko: 그는 가족을 위해 고귀한 희생을 했다.

noble.en: belonging to a family of high social rank, often with a title

ex.en: She married into a noble family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 귀족 가문으로 시집갔다.

noble.en: a person of high social rank, especially one with a title

ex.en: The nobles gathered at the palace for the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그 귀족들은 의식을 위해 궁전에 모였다.

nobleman.en: a man who belongs to the noble class, often with a title

ex.en: The nobleman owned vast estates across the country.
ex.ko: 그 귀족은 전국에 걸쳐 광대한 영지를 소유하고 있었다.

nomad.cue: 정착하지 않고 옮겨 다니며 사는 사람
nomad.en: a person who moves from place to place instead of living in one location

ex.en: The nomads moved their herds to find fresh grazing land.
ex.ko: 그 유목민들은 새로운 방목지를 찾아 가축을 이동시켰다.

nomination.en: the act of officially suggesting someone for a job, position, or award

ex.en: She received a nomination for Best Actress.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여우주연상 후보로 지명되었다.

nominee.en: a person who has been officially suggested for a job, position, or award

ex.en: All five nominees attended the award ceremony.
ex.ko: 다섯 명의 후보자 모두 시상식에 참석했다.

ex.en: We requested a non-smoking room at the hotel.
ex.ko: 우리는 호텔에서 금연 객실을 요청했다.

nonprofessional.en: not done by or relating to people who are trained experts in a particular field

ex.en: The play was performed by a nonprofessional cast.
ex.ko: 그 연극은 비전문 배우들이 공연했다.

ex.en: The birds migrate northward in spring.
ex.ko: 그 새들은 봄에 북쪽으로 이동한다.

northward.en: moving or facing toward the north

ex.en: We took the northward route to avoid traffic.
ex.ko: 우리는 교통 체증을 피하려고 북쪽으로 향하는 경로를 택했다.

nostril.en: either of the two openings in the nose through which you breathe

ex.en: He had a small piercing in his left nostril.
ex.ko: 그는 왼쪽 콧구멍에 작은 피어싱을 하고 있었다.

ex.en: The odds were reduced to almost nought.
ex.ko: 가능성이 거의 영에 가깝게 줄어들었다.

nourish.en: provide a person, animal, or plant with the food necessary for life and growth

ex.en: A balanced diet nourishes the body.
ex.ko: 균형 잡힌 식단은 몸에 영양분을 공급한다.

nourish.en: help a feeling, idea, or relationship to develop and grow stronger

ex.en: Their friendship was nourished by years of shared experiences.
ex.ko: 그들의 우정은 수년간의 공유된 경험으로 자라났다.

nourishment.en: food and other substances necessary for growth, health, and life

ex.en: Premature babies need extra nourishment.
ex.ko: 미숙아는 추가적인 영양분이 필요하다.

novelty.en: the quality of being new, different, and interesting

ex.en: The novelty of the idea attracted a lot of attention.
ex.ko: 그 아이디어의 참신함이 많은 관심을 끌었다.

novelty.en: a small, cheap object sold as a souvenir or decoration

ex.en: The shop sold novelties like keychains and magnets.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 열쇠고리와 자석 같은 기념품 소품을 팔았다.

nuisance.en: a person, thing, or situation that is annoying or causes trouble

ex.en: The constant noise from the construction site is a real nuisance.
ex.ko: 공사장에서 나는 끊임없는 소음은 정말 골칫거리다.

numerical.en: expressed in or relating to numbers

ex.en: Please enter your answer in numerical order.
ex.ko: 답을 숫자 순서대로 입력해 주세요.

numerical.en: relating to the ability to work with numbers

ex.en: The test measures numerical reasoning skills.
ex.ko: 그 시험은 수리적 추론 능력을 측정한다.

nursery.en: a room in a house for a baby or young child

ex.en: They decorated the nursery with soft pastel colors.
ex.ko: 그들은 아기방을 부드러운 파스텔 색으로 꾸몄다.

nursery.en: a place where babies and young children are cared for while their parents are at work

ex.en: She drops her son off at the nursery every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 아들을 어린이집에 맡긴다.

nut.en: a hard-shelled edible seed, such as an almond or walnut

ex.en: She sprinkled some chopped nuts on top of the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드 위에 다진 견과를 뿌렸다.

nut.en: a small piece of metal with a hole in the middle that screws onto a bolt to hold parts together

ex.en: He tightened the nut with a wrench.
ex.ko: 그는 렌치로 너트를 조였다.

nut.en: a person who is extremely enthusiastic about something (informal)

ex.en: My brother is a total fitness nut.
ex.ko: 내 남동생은 완전 헬스광이다.

nutshell.en: used to say that you are giving the basic facts about something without a lot of detail

ex.en: In a nutshell, the project was a success.
ex.ko: 한마디로 말해, 그 프로젝트는 성공적이었다.

nutshell.en: the hard outer covering of a nut

ex.en: He cracked open the nutshell to get to the walnut inside.
ex.ko: 그는 안에 있는 호두를 꺼내려고 견과 껍질을 깼다.

oath.cue: 엄숙하게 진실을 말하거나 의무를 다하겠다고 맹세
oath.en: a formal and serious promise, often made while swearing to tell the truth or fulfill a duty

ex.en: The witness took an oath before testifying in court.
ex.ko: 그 증인은 법정에서 증언하기 전에 선서를 했다.

obedience.en: the quality or act of doing what you are told or following the rules

ex.en: The soldiers were trained in strict obedience to their commanders.
ex.ko: 그 군인들은 지휘관에게 엄격히 복종하도록 훈련받았다.

obedient.en: willing to do what you are told to do

ex.en: The dog is very obedient and follows commands well.
ex.ko: 그 개는 매우 말을 잘 듣고 명령을 잘 따른다.

obey.en: do what someone tells you to do, or follow a rule or law

ex.en: Soldiers must obey orders without question.
ex.ko: 군인들은 의문 없이 명령에 따라야 한다.

obituary.en: a notice in a newspaper announcing someone's death, often with details of their life

ex.en: His obituary was published in the local newspaper.
ex.ko: 그의 부고는 지역 신문에 실렸다.

obligation.en: something you must do because of a law, rule, promise, or moral duty

ex.en: Employees have an obligation to follow safety regulations.
ex.ko: 직원들은 안전 규정을 따를 의무가 있다.

observer.en: a person who watches or notices something

ex.en: Casual observers might not notice the difference.
ex.ko: 무심코 지켜보는 사람은 그 차이를 알아채지 못할 수도 있다.

observer.en: an expert who watches and comments on a particular subject

ex.en: Political observers predict a close election.
ex.ko: 정치 논평가들은 이번 선거가 접전이 될 것으로 예상한다.

obsess.en: think about someone or something all the time so that you cannot think of anything else

ex.en: She obsesses over every small detail of her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자기 일의 사소한 부분 하나하나에 집착한다.

obsession.cue: 어떤 것에서 벗어나지 못하는 집착 상태
obsession.en: a state in which someone thinks about a person or thing all the time and cannot stop

ex.en: His obsession with winning affected his friendships.
ex.ko: 이기는 것에 대한 그의 집착이 우정에 영향을 미쳤다.

obsessive.en: thinking about something so much that it becomes unhealthy or extreme

ex.en: He became obsessive about keeping his house perfectly clean.
ex.ko: 그는 집을 완벽하게 깨끗이 유지하는 것에 강박적으로 집착하게 되었다.

ex.en: There was something odd about his behavior that day.
ex.ko: 그날 그의 행동에는 뭔가 이상한 점이 있었다.

odd.en: (of a number) not able to be divided exactly by two

ex.en: Three, five, and seven are odd numbers.
ex.ko: 3, 5, 7은 홀수이다.

odd.en: not matching another of the same kind

ex.en: He was wearing two odd socks.
ex.ko: 그는 짝이 안 맞는 양말 두 짝을 신고 있었다.

odd.cue: 숫자 뒤에 붙어 대략 그 이상을 뜻하는
odd.en: used after a number to show that the real number is a little more than stated

ex.en: She's been working here for twenty-odd years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이곳에서 20여 년째 일하고 있다.

offend.en: make someone feel upset or angry, often without intending to

ex.en: I didn't mean to offend you with my comment.
ex.ko: 제 말로 기분 상하게 할 생각은 아니었어요.

offender.en: a person who has committed a crime or broken a rule

ex.en: First-time offenders may receive a lighter sentence.
ex.ko: 초범은 더 가벼운 형을 받을 수 있다.

ex.en: Driving without a license is a serious offense.
ex.ko: 면허 없이 운전하는 것은 심각한 범죄이다.

offense.en: a feeling of anger caused by something someone says or does

ex.en: He took offense at her blunt comments.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 직설적인 말에 불쾌해했다.

offense.en: in sports, the team or players trying to score

ex.en: The team's offense scored three goals in the first half.
ex.ko: 그 팀의 공격진은 전반전에 세 골을 넣었다.

ex.en: I can't tell you the exact date offhand.
ex.ko: 지금 즉흥적으로는 정확한 날짜를 말씀드리기 어렵네요.

offhand.en: showing little interest or care, in a way that can seem rude

ex.en: His offhand remark hurt her feelings.
ex.ko: 그의 무심한 말 한마디가 그녀의 기분을 상하게 했다.

offshore.en: in the sea, not far from the coast

ex.en: They found oil in an offshore drilling site.
ex.ko: 그들은 앞바다의 시추 현장에서 석유를 발견했다.

offshore.cue: 세금 등을 피해 다른 나라에 두는
offshore.en: based in another country, especially to avoid tax or regulations

ex.en: The company moved its profits to an offshore account.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 수익을 역외 계좌로 옮겼다.

ointment.en: a smooth substance that you rub on your skin to treat a cut, burn, or other problem

ex.en: Apply this ointment to the wound twice a day.
ex.ko: 이 연고를 상처에 하루 두 번 발라 주세요.

olive.en: a small oval fruit, green or black, used in cooking or to make oil

ex.en: She added some olives to the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드에 올리브를 좀 넣었다.

ex.en: He wore an olive green jacket.
ex.ko: 그는 올리브색 재킷을 입고 있었다.

Olympia.en: an ancient site in Greece where the original Olympic Games were held

ex.en: Olympia was the site of the ancient Olympic Games.
ex.ko: 올림피아는 고대 올림픽 경기가 열렸던 곳이다.

Olympiad.en: the Olympic Games, held once every four years

ex.en: Thousands of athletes competed in the Olympiad.
ex.ko: 수천 명의 선수들이 올림픽 대회에 참가했다.

Olympiad.en: an international competition in a particular academic subject, such as math or science

ex.en: She won a gold medal at the International Math Olympiad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 국제 수학 올림피아드에서 금메달을 땄다.

openly.en: in a way that is not secret or hidden; honestly

ex.en: She spoke openly about her struggles with anxiety.
ex.ko: 그녀는 불안 장애와의 싸움에 대해 공개적으로 이야기했다.

operator.en: a person whose job is to connect telephone calls or answer phone inquiries

ex.en: I asked the operator to connect me to the sales department.
ex.ko: 나는 교환원에게 영업부로 연결해 달라고 요청했다.

operator.en: a person or company that owns or runs a business, or someone who works a machine

ex.en: The tour operator arranged all our hotel bookings.
ex.ko: 그 여행사 운영자는 우리의 모든 호텔 예약을 처리해 주었다.

opponent.en: a person who is against you in a fight, game, or argument

ex.en: She easily defeated her opponent in the final match.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결승전에서 상대를 쉽게 이겼다.

optimism.en: a feeling of hope and confidence that things will turn out well

ex.en: Despite the setback, she maintained a sense of optimism.
ex.ko: 차질에도 불구하고 그녀는 낙관적인 마음을 유지했다.

optimist.en: a person who tends to expect good things to happen

ex.en: As an optimist, he always sees the bright side of things.
ex.ko: 낙관주의자로서 그는 항상 매사의 밝은 면을 본다.

optimistic.en: hopeful and confident that good things will happen

ex.en: She feels optimistic about her chances of getting the job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 일자리를 얻을 가능성에 대해 낙관적으로 느낀다.

optional.en: available to be chosen but not required

ex.en: Attendance at the meeting is optional.
ex.ko: 그 회의 참석은 선택 사항이다.

orator.en: a person who is skilled at public speaking

ex.en: He was known as a powerful orator who could move crowds.
ex.ko: 그는 대중을 감동시킬 수 있는 강력한 연설가로 알려져 있었다.

orchestral.en: relating to or written for an orchestra

ex.en: The film features a beautiful orchestral score.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 아름다운 관현악 음악을 특징으로 한다.

orchid.en: a plant with brightly colored, unusually shaped flowers

ex.en: She received a bouquet of orchids for her birthday.
ex.ko: 그녀는 생일에 난초 꽃다발을 받았다.

organizer.en: a person who plans and arranges an event or activity

ex.en: The event organizer thanked all the volunteers.
ex.ko: 그 행사 주최자는 모든 자원봉사자에게 감사를 표했다.

originality.en: the quality of being new and different from anything that already exists

ex.en: The judges praised the originality of her design.
ex.ko: 심사위원들은 그녀의 디자인의 독창성을 칭찬했다.

originally.en: at first, or in the beginning, before something changed

ex.en: The building was originally used as a factory.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 원래 공장으로 쓰였다.

originate.en: begin or come from a particular place, time, or situation

ex.en: The custom is believed to have originated in ancient China.
ex.ko: 그 관습은 고대 중국에서 유래된 것으로 여겨진다.

ornament.en: an object used to make something look more attractive

ex.en: They hung ornaments on the Christmas tree.
ex.ko: 그들은 크리스마스트리에 장식품을 걸었다.

ornament.en: make something more attractive by adding decorative objects

ex.en: The garden was ornamented with small statues.
ex.ko: 그 정원은 작은 조각상들로 장식되어 있었다.

ounce.en: a unit of weight equal to about 28 grams

ex.en: The recipe calls for eight ounces of flour.
ex.ko: 그 레시피는 밀가루 8온스를 필요로 한다.

outback.en: the remote inland areas of Australia, away from cities

ex.en: They drove across the outback for three days.
ex.ko: 그들은 사흘 동안 오지를 가로질러 운전했다.

outback.en: remote and not many people live there

ex.en: They lived in an outback town far from any city.
ex.ko: 그들은 어느 도시에서도 먼 외딴 마을에 살았다.

outbreak.en: a sudden start of something unpleasant, such as a disease or violence

ex.en: The outbreak of the disease spread quickly across the region.
ex.ko: 그 질병의 발생은 지역 전체로 빠르게 퍼졌다.

outcome.en: the final result of a process or action

ex.en: We are waiting to hear the outcome of the negotiations.
ex.ko: 우리는 협상 결과를 기다리고 있다.

outdistance.en: go much faster or further ahead than someone else, especially in a race or competition

ex.en: The runner quickly outdistanced the rest of the field.
ex.ko: 그 선수는 나머지 선수들을 빠르게 훨씬 앞질렀다.

outdo.en: do something better than someone else

ex.en: Each team tried to outdo the others in the competition.
ex.ko: 각 팀은 경쟁에서 서로를 능가하려 애썼다.

outfit.cue: 특정 상황에 맞춰 갖춰 입은 옷
outfit.en: a set of clothes worn together, especially for a particular occasion

ex.en: She bought a new outfit for the wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결혼식을 위해 새 옷차림을 샀다.

outfit.en: provide someone or something with equipment for a particular purpose

ex.en: The expedition was outfitted with the latest survival gear.
ex.ko: 그 탐험대는 최신 생존 장비를 갖췄다.

outgrow.cue: 몸이나 마음이 자라서 예전 것이 안 맞게
outgrow.ko: 몸이 커져서 안 맞게 되다, (습관을) 벗어나다
outgrow.en: grow too big for your clothes, or grow out of a habit or interest as you get older

ex.en: She quickly outgrew her old shoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 금세 자라서 예전 신발이 안 맞게 되었다.

outing.en: a short trip taken for pleasure or leisure

ex.en: The class went on a fun outing to the zoo.
ex.ko: 그 학급은 동물원으로 즐거운 나들이를 갔다.

outlet.en: a place in a wall where you can plug in an electrical device

ex.en: Is there an outlet near the desk for my laptop?
ex.ko: 책상 근처에 노트북을 꽂을 콘센트가 있나요?

outlet.en: a shop or store that sells goods, often at a discount

ex.en: The brand has retail outlets in every major city.
ex.ko: 그 브랜드는 모든 주요 도시에 판매점을 두고 있다.

outlet.en: a way of expressing your feelings or energy

ex.en: Running became an outlet for her stress.
ex.ko: 달리기는 그녀의 스트레스 배출구가 되었다.

outlive.en: live longer than someone or something else

ex.en: She outlived her husband by twenty years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남편보다 20년을 더 오래 살았다.

outlook.en: a person's general attitude toward life and the world

ex.en: He has a positive outlook on life.
ex.ko: 그는 인생에 대해 긍정적인 관점을 가지고 있다.

outlook.en: what is expected to happen in the future

ex.en: The economic outlook for next year looks promising.
ex.ko: 내년 경제 전망은 밝아 보인다.

outpatient.en: a patient who receives treatment at a hospital but does not stay overnight

ex.en: He was treated as an outpatient after the minor surgery.
ex.ko: 그는 가벼운 수술 후 외래 환자로 치료받았다.

output.cue: 일정 기간 동안 만들어 낸 양
output.en: the amount of something produced by a person, machine, or country

ex.en: The factory increased its output by 20 percent.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 생산량을 20퍼센트 늘렸다.

output.en: the signal or power that comes out of an electronic device or system

ex.en: Check the audio output settings on your computer.
ex.ko: 컴퓨터의 오디오 출력 설정을 확인해 보세요.

outrage.en: a strong feeling of anger and shock caused by something unfair or wrong

ex.en: The decision caused public outrage.
ex.ko: 그 결정은 대중의 격분을 일으켰다.

outrage.en: a shocking, cruel, or unacceptable act

ex.en: The attack on civilians was described as an outrage.
ex.ko: 민간인에 대한 그 공격은 만행이라고 표현되었다.

ex.en: It was outrageous that he was allowed to leave without punishment.
ex.ko: 그가 처벌 없이 떠나도록 허용된 것은 터무니없었다.

outrageous.en: much more than is reasonable, especially in price or amount

ex.en: The hotel charged an outrageous price for a simple breakfast.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 간단한 아침 식사에 터무니없는 가격을 매겼다.

outshine.en: be much better than someone else and get more attention or praise

ex.en: Her performance outshone all the other contestants.
ex.ko: 그녀의 공연은 다른 모든 참가자들을 능가했다.
